title: Synthesis of in vitro transcribed RNA from whole bacterial transcriptome
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aa8ol","text":"Identifying RNA modifications across the transcriptome has been made possible by direct RNA sequencing using nanopore sequencing from Oxford Nanopore Technologies. This is due to voltage changes happening when each nucleotide passes through the pore. Hypothesis is that since each nucleotide passing through the pore has a distinct change in voltage, nucleotides with epigenetic modifications (e.g.methylation) also has distinct voltage changes when passing through. This is identified by errors in basecalling.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7an1p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vepd","text":"Several software have been developed to identify locations of modifications using machine learning, deep learning models. In order to make highly accurate predictions, there is the necessity of comparing the signals from native RNA sequencing with RNA without any modifications. A few recent research publications get RNA through in vitro transcription but only for ribosomal RNAs (rRNA). This is easier since there are only 7 different ribosomal RNAs for each type i.e., 16S, 23S and 5S. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":330,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bbevl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6tcn9","text":"Getting IVT RNA for entire transcriptome is not trivial since bacterial transcriptome is complex and depends on the condition the bacteria has been grown in. We use the method of strand switching during cDNA synthesis to get cDNA for whole bacterial transcriptome which can then be used as a template for IVT.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bmacm","text":"Great precautions should be taken when handling RNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"de10o","text":"Clean the bench surfaces and pipettes using RNase ZAP to inhibit all RNases.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9cb73","text":"Always use RNase free centrifuge tubes, PCR tubes and pipette tips for all steps wherever required.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

R1013","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c9fm7","text":"RNaseOUT","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6m4cm","text":"Primers: oligo-d(T)-22 primer, T7-Strand Switch Primer, PR2/T7 promoter primer (primer sequences commented in the protocol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4npdo","text":"10 mM dNTP solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b18os","text":"Nuclease-free water (Thermofisher - #AM9937)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ctnt9","text":"0.2 mL thin-walled PCR tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7dc6r","text":"1.5 mL Eppendorf LoBind tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6evlm","text":"Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase (200 U/uL) with 5x RT buffer (Thermofisher - #EP0751)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3r3r","text":"Pre-chilled freezer block at -20 C","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9hsh9","text":"LongAmp Taq 2X MasterMix (NEB - #M0287)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"buvit","text":"RNase Cocktail Enzyme Mix (Thermofisher - #AM2286)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9i0n","text":"RNAclean Agencourt XP beads","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cnsld","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eo8f9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: A reaction is setup in a PCR tube using the following components A B C D E F G H 1 Components Volume (µL) 2 RNA 1 - 10 µg 3 10x E. coli poly(A) polymerase reaction buffer 2 4 ATP (10 mM) 2 5 RNase inhibitor 1 6 E. coli poly(A) polymerase 1 7 Water upto 25 µL 8 Total 25
Incubate the reaction mixture for 00:15:00 at 37 °C in a thermocycler followed by 00:20:00 at 65 °C for inactivation.

Transfer the reaction mixture into a RNase free tube for cleanup step. Add 45 µL, (1.8 volumes of reaction) of RNAClean AMPure XP beads and 105 µL, (4.2 volumes) of 100 % ethanol to the reaction mixture
Flick the tube for mixing and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Keep the tube on a magnetic rack and allow the beads to pellet towards the magnet
Prepare 500 µL of 80% ethanol during this time. Pipette the solution out of the tube taking care to not disturb the pellet
Wash the beads by pipetting 150 µL of 80% ethanol and repeat the step. Remove the ethanol, spin and put the tube back in the magnetic rack to remove any left over solution
Allow the beads to air dry for 00:00:30 making sure not to over dry. Add 20 µL of RNase free water to the beads and incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 to elute RNA.
Place the tube back on the magnetic rack allowing the beads to separate and pipette the poly(A) tailed RNA in water into a new RNase free tube and keep it On ice
Prepare a reaction with the following components in a PCR tube A B 1 Reagent Volume 2 poly A+ RNA 1 µg (x µL) 3 anchored oligo d(T)22 1 µL 4 10 mM dNTPs 1 µL 5 RNase free water 9 - x µL 6 Total 11 µL
Incubate the reaction mixture at 65 °C for 00:05:00 in a thermocycler and then snap cool on a pre-chilled freezer block
In a separate tube, mix together the following: %s %s Reagent Volume 5x RT Buffer 4 μl RNaseOUT 1 μl Nuclease-free water 1 μl Strand-Switching Primer (SSP) 2 μl Total 8 μl
Mix gently by flicking the tube , and spin down .
Add the strand-switching buffer to the snap-cooled, annealed mRNA, mix by flicking the tube and spin down
Incubate at 42 °C for 00:02:00
Add 1 µl of Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase. The total volume is now 20 µl.
Mix gently by flicking the tube , and spin down .
Incubate using the following protocol: %s %s Cycle step Temperature Time No. of cycles Reverse transcription and strand-switching 42° C 90 mins 1 Heat inactivation 85° C 5 mins 1 Hold 4° C ∞
Add 1 µL of RNase Cocktail Enzyme Mix (ThermoFisher, AM2286) to the reverse transcription reaction.
Incubate the reaction for 00:10:00 at 37 °C .
Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing.
Transfer the sample to a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 17 µL of resuspended AMPure XP beads to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube .
Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Prepare 500 µL of fresh 70% ethanol in nuclease-free water.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet . Keep the tube on the magnet, and pipette off the supernatant.
Keep the tube on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 70% ethanol without disturbing the pellet. Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard.
Repeat the previous step.
Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 , but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 20 µL nuclease-free water.
Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless.
Remove and retain 20 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.

Prepare the following reaction in a 0.2 ml thin-walled PCR tube: %s %s Reagent Volume 2x LongAmp Taq Master Mix 25 μl PR2 Primer (PR2) 2 μl Reverse-transcribed sample from above 20 μl Nuclease-free water 3 μl Total 50 μl
Incubate using the following protocol: %s %s Temperature Time No. of cycles 94 °C 1 mins 1 50 °C 1 mins 1 65 °C 15 mins 1 4 °C ∞
Resuspend the AMPure XP beads by vortexing.
Transfer the sample to a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Add 40 µL of resuspended AMPure XP beads to the reaction and mix by flicking the tube .
Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Prepare 500 µL of fresh 70% ethanol in nuclease-free water.
Spin down the sample and pellet on a magnet . Keep the tube on the magnet, and pipette off the supernatant.
Keep the tube on the magnet and wash the beads with 200 µL of freshly prepared 70% ethanol without disturbing the pellet. Remove the ethanol using a pipette and discard.
Repeat the previous step.
Spin down and place the tube back on the magnet. Pipette off any residual ethanol. Allow to dry for ~ 00:00:30 , but do not dry the pellet to the point of cracking.
Remove the tube from the magnetic rack and resuspend pellet in 21 µl nuclease-free water.
Incubate on a Hula mixer (rotator mixer) for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Pellet beads on magnet until the eluate is clear and colourless.
Remove and retain 21 µL of eluate into a clean 1.5 ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube.
Measure the concentration of the double stranded cDNA obtained after this step.
100 ng of double stranded cDNA synthesized is used for in vitro transcription.
New England Biolab's HiScribe™ T7 Quick High Yield RNA Synthesis Kit (E2050s) is used for RNA synthesis using cDNA as a template.
Thaw the necessary kit components, mix and pulse-spin in microfuge to collect solutions to the bottoms of tubes. Keep on ice.

Assemble the reaction at room temperature in the following order: A B 1 Components Volume 2 Nuclease-free water used to make up the volume to 20 μL 3 NTP Buffer Mix 10 μL (10 mM each NTP final) 4 Template DNA (cDNA) X μL (100 ng - 1 μg) 5 T7 RNA Polymerase Mix 2 μL 6 Total Reaction Volume 20 μL
Mix thoroughly by pipetting and pulse-spin in a microfuge.
Incubate at 37 °C for 02:00:00 . Incubation over 2 hours will no negatively affect the RNA synthesis
30 µL of Nuclease-free water to the reaction mixture and 2 µL of DNase I (included in the kit) is added after that.
Mix the reaction mixture by pipetting and incubate for 00:15:00 at 37 °C
Following the incubation, RNA is purified using any RNA cleanup kit. I used the RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 kit (R1013) and finally eluted in 20 µL of RNase-free water.
Concentration is checked using Qubit RNA kit.
creator: Bhargava Reddy Morampalli
